text.title: Secrets Rotation End-to-End Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Secrets Rotation End-to-End application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Cadence: database passwords 90d, third-party API keys 180d, signing keys 365d, JWT secrets 90d, OAuth client secrets 180d. Lower cadence for higher blast radius.
rule.rationale: Without cadence policy, secrets rotate ad-hoc + most never rotate; explicit table covers the inventory.

rule.statement: Rotation = create new secret + dual-active period (24-72h) + retire old. App must accept both during window.
rule.rationale: Hard-cutover rotations cause downtime; dual-secret window enables zero-downtime rotation.

rule.statement: Apps reload secrets without restart (file watch, HUP signal, manager SDK). Restart-required rotation = scheduled downtime each rotation.
rule.rationale: Restart-required rotation discourages rotation; reload-no-restart enables on-demand emergency rotation.

rule.statement: Every rotation logged: who, when, what secret, old + new version IDs (NOT values). Immutable + retained per compliance.
rule.rationale: Audit trail is the only post-incident evidence of clean state; mutable logs fail audit.

rule.statement: Runbook for emergency rotation (suspected leak): immediate dual-secret with 1-hour window + parallel forensics. Practice quarterly.
rule.rationale: Real leaks compress dual-secret window; without practice, emergency rotation fails.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
